mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real retail sales proxy RSAFS signals near-term consumption strength and improving earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 447308.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher trailing 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in walk-forward tests, or if top-quintile RSAFS momentum has lower mean 20-day returns than bottom-quintile momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average by more than 50 basis points, discount-rate pressure dominates earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average by > 0.50pp
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 0.38 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered returns are not lower than non-triggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase crossing above +0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deterioration in labor-market income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 4.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration periods produce non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, policy is behind the inflation curve and equity valuation multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 0.38 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with policy behind curve
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has no more negative 20-day returns than CPIAUCSL acceleration alone, or if the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL growth and low FEDFUNDS is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with a falling UNRATE signals synchronized production and labor demand expansion, raising expected cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 106.8477 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform periods with only rising INDPRO by at least 25 basis points over 60 trading days on average, or if the 60-day Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST decline crossing below -10% year over year signals weakening housing-linked demand and tighter household balance-sheet conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 1099.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth with a non-rising UNRATE indicates payroll expansion without rising unemployment stress, supporting aggregate income and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 143560.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal has a 20-day positive-return hit rate below 50%, or if its average 20-day return is not higher than the average return from PAYEMS growth alone.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL growth rate crossing above its trailing 12-month average signals inflation news that raises expected policy tightening and lowers equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly growth crosses above its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL snapshot before 2021-09-15 was 238.107 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration periods produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if triggered mean 5-day returns are not below non-triggered mean returns.
